source_test: HEC.FDA.ModelTest/unittests/PerformanceTest.cs (AssuranceResultStorageShould is the RNG-conformance source for case 2; cases 1 and 3 are pure-numeric SystemPerformanceResults-level oracles that don't need the full ImpactAreaScenarioSimulation, modeled on the same file's ComputeLeveeAEP_Test fragility curve for case 3 -- no upstream unit test exercises AEP/LTEP or the levee path at the SystemPerformanceResults level directly)
note: Three case shapes selected by `construct.case_kind`, matching EvalSystemPerformanceResults in Program.cs and run_system_performance_results in test_fixtures.cpp case-for-case. (1) 'aep' -- SystemPerformanceResults(ConvergenceCriteria(min,max)), stages `aep_observations` ({iteration,result}) via AddAEPForAssurance, one PutDataIntoHistograms() call, then MeanAEP/MedianAEP/LongTermExceedanceProbability(years); only 8 of the 100-slot staging array are written (rest default to 0), matching AssuranceResultStorage's own multi_iteration_partial fixture convention. (2) 'rng_conformance' -- the PerformanceTest.AssuranceResultStorageShould pin: SystemPerformanceResults(ConvergenceCriteria(1000,10000)) (IterationCount defaults to 100, MaxIterations>=100), AddStageAssuranceHistogram(standard_probability), then `master_seed`-seeded `Random.Next()` reproduces the real test's masterSeedList loop (seeding IterationCount=100 master seeds -- the real Parallel.For only ever consumes the first IterationCount of the 1000-element seeds array, so this is an exact-prefix-equivalent simplification); for `compute_chunks` outer passes, each of the `iteration_count` seeds feeds RandomProvider(seed).NextRandom() -> standard Normal InverseCDF -> AddStageForAssurance, with one PutDataIntoHistograms() per pass (10000 total observations: the same 100 deterministic values repeated 100 times, since the inner computation never depends on the outer pass index j). assurance_of_event proves the ported DotNetRandom(1234)->RandomProvider->Normal.InverseCDF chain reproduces the real C# bit-for-bit; normal_cdf_reference is the same Normal(0,1).CDF(threshold_value) PerformanceTest.cs compares assurance_of_event against (tol .009 there), pinned from the identical C# run for an apples-to-apples cross-check. (3) 'levee' -- SystemPerformanceResults(UncertainPairedData(system_response_xs, Deterministic(system_response_ys)), ConvergenceCriteria(min,max)) with system_response_xs/ys reproducing PerformanceTest.ComputeLeveeAEP_Test's StageForNonLeveeFailureProbs/ProbLeveeFailure fragility curve verbatim (a wide, >2-point curve so the >2-Xvals-length ctor check sets _CalculatePerformanceForLevee=true); AddStageAssuranceHistogram(standard_probability) + `stage_observations` (10 of the 100-slot staging array written, rest default 0) exercise CalculateAssuranceForLevee's FP-sensitive cumulative-exceedance-times-fragility integration loop. assurance_of_event's threshold_value arg is passed but IGNORED by the levee branch (see system_performance_results.hpp's class comment) -- kept in the construct/args shape purely for interface parity with case 1.
